content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Advice File                                Date : Nov. 14, 1979


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Sarah Cameron

Subject: Effect of Supreme Court's decision in IGA

I got a call today from Thad Howard in Speaker McCarthy's
Los Angeles Office. He said McCarthy was getting ready to send out
a solicitation letter signed by several labor leaders, including
John Henning, who is a registered lobbyist. He asked if there
was any problem with this in light of IGA. I told him that our
application for a stay of the Court's decision was still pending
so that the decision is in effect and consequently the affected
provisions of the Act are not. Therefore, it is not illegal to
do this. He asked what would happen if the stay is later granted -
would there be any retroactive effect to the stay. I said no,
that actions taken during the period that the decision was in
effect would still be legal, but that of course they would have to
stop using the letter or taking any other action in violation of
the sections which would be put back into effect by the stay.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a solicitation letter signed by a registered lobbyist (John Henning) in connection with Speaker McCarthy's fundraising is permissible under the Political Reform Act in light of the Supreme Court's IGA decision.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that because the Court's IGA decision is currently in effect and the FPPC's application for a stay is still pending, the affected provisions of the Act are not in effect, making such solicitation legal at present. However, if the stay is later granted, actions taken during the period when the decision was in effect would remain legal, but future violations of reinstated provisions would need to cease.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal memorandum/correspondence documenting oral advice rather than a formal advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document uses informal format. Confidence reduced due to lack of standard advice letter structure and some ambiguity about the specific IGA case reference.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a solicitation letter signed by a registered lobbyist (John Henning) in connection with Speaker McCarthy's fundraising is permissible under the Political Reform Act in light of the Supreme Court's IGA decision.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that because the Court's IGA decision is currently in effect and the FPPC's application for a stay is still pending, the affected provisions of the Act are not in effect, making such solicitation legal at present. However, if the stay is later granted, actions taken during the period when the decision was in effect would remain legal, but future violations of reinstated provisions would need to cease.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : Advice File Date : Nov. 14, 1979 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Sarah Cameron Subject: Effect of Supreme Court's decision in IGA I got a call today from Thad Howard in Speaker McCarthy's Los Angeles Office. He said McCarthy was getting ready to send out a solicitation letter signed by several labor leaders, including John Henning, who is a registered lobbyist. He asked if there was any problem with this in light of IGA. I told him that our application for a stay of the Court's decision was still pending so that the decision is in effect and consequently the affected provisions of the Act are not. Therefore, it is not illegal to do this. He asked what would happen if the stay is later granted - would there be any retroactive effect to the stay. I said no, that actions taken during the period that the decision was in effect would still be legal, but that of course they would have to stop using the letter or taking any other action in violation of the sections which would be put back into effect by the stay.
embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum documenting a telephone advice call regarding whether a labor leader and registered lobbyist can sign a solicitation letter for Speaker McCarthy in light of the Supreme Court's IGA decision affecting the Political Reform Act.
